title: Showing Off / Ostentation (Riya)
subtitle: The Hidden Shirk That Nullifies Deeds

verses.arabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُصَلِّينَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ عَن صَلَاتِهِمْ سَاهُونَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ يُرَاءُونَ
verses.translation: So woe to those who pray — [but] who are heedless of their prayer — those who make show [of their deeds].

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يُخَادِعُونَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ خَادِعُهُمْ وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ يُرَاءُونَ النَّاسَ وَلَا يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
verses.translation: Indeed, the hypocrites [think to] deceive Allah, but He is deceiving them. And when they stand for prayer, they stand lazily, showing [themselves to] the people and not remembering Allah except a little.

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تُبْطِلُوا صَدَقَاتِكُم بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذَىٰ كَالَّذِي يُنفِقُ مَالَهُ رِئَاءَ النَّاسِ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, do not invalidate your charities with reminders [of it] or injury as does one who spends his wealth [only] to be seen by the people.

brief_explanation: Riya is performing acts of worship — prayer, charity, fasting, recitation — for the eyes of people rather than for Allah alone. The Quran links riya directly to the munafiqeen, who stand for prayer lazily while showing themselves to people. Surah Al-Ma'un delivers a devastating verdict: woe (wayl, a valley in Hell) to those who pray but are heedless and perform acts of show. The Prophet (peace be upon him) called riya 'the minor shirk' — because the deed is directed to other than Allah, nullifying its reward entirely. On the Day of Judgment, Allah will say to the one who performed deeds for show: 'Go to those for whom you showed off and seek your reward from them.'
capital_punishment: There is no worldly hadd for riya as it is primarily a sin of the heart and intention. It is addressed through sincere tawbah and purifying one's intention (ikhlas) to be entirely for Allah. If riya is coupled with acts that harm others (e.g., false charity that deceives communities), ta'zir may be applied for the deception. The true punishment is described in the Quran: nullified deeds and, for persistent riya combined with nifaq, the prospect of the lowest pit of Hell.
